name: Taleju Bhawani
description: Tutelary goddess of the Malla kings. Her temples stand in all three Durbar Squares. Only the king (and now, selected priests) may enter the inner sanctum.
note: Secret tantric deity whose rituals are closely guarded by the Karmacharya priests

name: Machhindranath
description: Rain god and patron deity of the Kathmandu Valley. Worshipped by both Hindus (as Avalokiteshvara/Lokeshwor) and Buddhists. Central to the Rato Machindranath Jatra.
alternate_names: Rato Machindranath, Bunga Dyo, Lokeshwor, Avalokiteshvara
note: One of the rare deities worshipped equally by Hindus and Buddhists in Nepal

name: Bhairav
description: Fierce manifestation of Shiva. Akash Bhairav (sky Bhairav) is displayed during Indra Jatra. Kal Bhairav's stone image at Hanuman Dhoka is a famous Kathmandu landmark.
alternate_names: Akash Bhairav, Kal Bhairav, Swet Bhairav, Bhailo
note: Multiple forms of Bhairav guard different parts of the Kathmandu Valley

name: Kumari
description: Living goddess believed to be the incarnation of Taleju (Durga). A pre-pubescent girl from the Newar Shakya clan is selected through rigorous tests and serves until puberty.
note: The Kumari tradition exists in Kathmandu, Patan, and Bhaktapur

name: Manjushri
description: Bodhisattva of wisdom. Legend says Manjushri drained the ancient lake of the Kathmandu Valley with a sword cut at Chobar, making it habitable.
note: His mythological act of draining the valley lake is central to Kathmandu's origin story

name: Ganesh
description: Elephant-headed god of beginnings and remover of obstacles. Ashok Binayak at Kathmandu Durbar Square is one of the Valley's four main Ganesh shrines.
note: The four protective Ganesh shrines mark the cardinal directions of old Kathmandu

name: White Tara
description: Female Bodhisattva of compassion and longevity. Identified with Bhrikuti, the Nepali princess who married Tibetan king Songtsen Gampo.
note: Bhrikuti is venerated as Green Tara in Tibet but White Tara in Nepal

name: Pashupatinath
description: Supreme form of Shiva as Lord of Animals. The main deity of the Pashupatinath Temple — Nepal's most sacred Hindu site.
note: The lingam inside the temple is said to have four faces (chaturmukha)
